description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a green camouflage paint scheme and large yellow identification numbers '633' on both wings. The fuselage is streamlined, with a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring a clear canopy. The wings are straight and slightly angled downward, with visible underwing attachments that may represent bombs or fuel tanks. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel, all clearly visible.
description: A green, single-propeller military airplane with yellow wing markings, a forward enclosed cockpit, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, forward cockpit canopy, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single propeller, underwing attachments, and the downward angle of the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the full span of the wings with yellow markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and underwing details from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and slight anhedral angle, no winglets or variable geometry.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy integrated into the fuselage.
